title: k_thread_abort leaves RX callback in undefined state
description:
lora_l2.c:339-340: After k_thread_abort(), the RX thread is terminated mid-execution. If the thread was inside lora_recv() or the callback invocation, resources may be leaked or mutex left locked.

What goes wrong:
1. If thread was in callback (lora_rx_callback -> lichen_l2_input), rx_mutex could be locked. lichen_l2_enable(true) would deadlock.
2. If thread was in lora_recv(), the LoRa driver's internal state may be corrupted.
3. Any in-flight net_pkt allocation may be leaked.

The code does clear the callback at lines 315-316 BEFORE signaling exit, but k_thread_abort() can happen while the callback is executing (after the callback pointer was snapshotted at line 191-193).

Fix: After abort, explicitly reinitialize rx_mutex. Or document that abort is a last resort and the interface requires full re-init after an abort. Consider adding a flag that prevents re-start after forced abort.
close_reason: Fixed: Added aborted flag to track forced thread abort, added lichen_lora_l2_deinit() to reinitialize mutex after abort, start() now checks aborted flag and returns -ECANCELED if set, added lichen_lora_l2_needs_reinit() query function
